channel.title: Ai on Maven AI
channel.description: Recent content in Ai on Maven AI

item.title: No Extra Cost. Free, Yes Free: How We Cut AI Spending 60% in One Afternoon
item.description: From $20/day to $8/day in 4 hours. Zero new expenses. Just smart architecture and open-source tools.

item.title: Building a Local AI Memory System in 90 Minutes (Zero Cost, No Vendor Lock-In)
item.description: How I went from research to a working local AI memory system inspired by Hindsight, Mem0, and GitHub Copilot—completely free, fully private, no subscriptions.

item.title: AI Memory Systems in 2026: Why Your Agent Keeps Forgetting Everything
item.description: Most AI agents have amnesia. Here's what the latest research says about fixing it—and why the difference between conversation memory and institutional knowledge matters more than you think.

item.title: Agent Platforms Are Commoditizing — Should You Build On One or Own Your Stack?
item.description:
If you’re building an AI product right now, you’ve probably heard the pitch: “Use our agent platform. Build AI agents without code. Ship faster.”

Bitta, Relevance AI, n8n, Dust, LangChain, Zapier AI, OpenClaw — everyone’s selling agent orchestration platforms. And they all promise the same thing: speed, simplicity, and no engineering required.

But here’s the question nobody’s asking:

Should you lock into an abstraction layer when the real value is in your domain logic, not their infrastructure?

item.title: How We Built an Autonomous AI Partnership in One Night
item.description: A step-by-step breakdown of establishing trust, autonomy, and real working boundaries with an AI assistant
